title: The Fool Monty

jokes.text: NBC, you are here to listen and not speak!
jokes.setup: Secret media conclave meeting
jokes.punchline: NBC being relegated to silent observer status
jokes.explanation: Sharp jab at NBC's declining status in media hierarchy, economical and pointed

jokes.text: It's like the summer of the shark, except instead of a shark, it's an epidemic... and instead of summer, it's all the time!
jokes.setup: Comparing new health scare to past media hysteria
jokes.punchline: Convoluted analogy that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Satirizes media's tendency to manufacture crises with increasingly ridiculous comparisons

jokes.text: He loves being that guy.
jokes.setup: Executive saying he hates to derail what everyone loves
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction revealing his true nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words completely undermine the previous statement

jokes.text: The only moral thing to do is release a deadly virus into the general public.
jokes.setup: Discussion of ethics in creating health scares
jokes.punchline: Suggesting actual bioterrorism as the 'moral' option
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of corporate ethics - framing mass murder as morally superior to lying

jokes.text: Get over here, NBC. (chuckling) Well, we certainly believe in testing, but I... Oh! (woozy moan)
jokes.setup: NBC being called over as test subject
jokes.punchline: Immediate infection from untested virus
jokes.explanation: Payoff to NBC's diminished status - they become the guinea pig

jokes.text: that warm body on your lap just might be ready to destroy your tender vittles
jokes.setup: Warning about cats spreading disease
jokes.punchline: Using 'vittles' (food) to mean vital organs
jokes.explanation: Wordplay mixing folksy language with medical terminology for humorous effect

jokes.text: mild thirst, occasional hunger, tiredness at night
jokes.setup: Listing symptoms of dangerous disease
jokes.punchline: Symptoms that describe normal human existence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of vague medical warnings - symptoms so broad they apply to everyone

jokes.text: We have to burn everything the cat touched, before the virus makes us paranoid!
jokes.setup: Homer responding to health scare
jokes.punchline: Warning about paranoia while acting extremely paranoid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - complete lack of self-awareness about his own paranoid behavior

jokes.text: I thought he was an old clump of rags.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer knew Bart was in the burning pile
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal of potentially burning his son
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - treating near-infanticide as minor mistake

jokes.text: Please use your time in line wisely to 'Sophie's Choice' your child.
jokes.setup: Announcement about limited vaccines
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing Holocaust film about impossible parental choice
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant reference - using 'Sophie's Choice' as a verb for an impossible decision

jokes.text: I don't even have a kid that I admit to having.
jokes.setup: Complaining about choosing which child gets vaccine
jokes.punchline: Revealing he has children but denies them
jokes.explanation: Dark throwaway line revealing character's deadbeat parent status

jokes.text: Because, you see, I am afraid of needles, but Wolverine is not. Snikt! Snikt!
jokes.setup: Bart wearing Wolverine costume in vaccine line
jokes.punchline: Child logic about costume giving him superpowers
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic - costume as psychological armor against fear

jokes.text: Need... acceptable currency... Ooh... for delicious treat... Ooh!
jokes.setup: Burns hearing ice cream truck
jokes.punchline: Billionaire reduced to wanting ice cream but having no small bills
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - ultra-wealthy Burns reduced to basic human desire

jokes.text: One for me, one for Smithers here and the rest for my hounds.
jokes.setup: Burns ordering 37 vaccines
jokes.punchline: Treating his dogs better than humans while Smithers gets equal treatment
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns - treating dogs and Smithers as equals, both above regular people

jokes.text: Give him what he wants. He's the only taxpayer in this town.
jokes.setup: People objecting to Burns getting special treatment
jokes.punchline: Revealing the economic reality of Springfield's tax base
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economic satire - explains why corrupt mayors protect the wealthy

jokes.text: They'll see me take it and they'll want some. Yes 'oo will. Yes 'oo will, you little jelly belly.
jokes.setup: Objection that dogs can't get the disease
jokes.punchline: Burns talking baby talk to dogs while ignoring human suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character work - showing more affection to dogs than humans

jokes.text: Hurry, everyone! Roll in the shards! Come on! Like this!
jokes.setup: Burns crashes through window
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking broken glass has medicinal properties
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic - broken glass from wealthy person must be beneficial

jokes.text: Healthy. Gettin' healthy. Ow! Gettin' healthy... and very sleepy.
jokes.setup: Homer rolling in broken glass
jokes.punchline: Maintaining delusion while clearly being injured and losing consciousness
jokes.explanation: Homer's stubborn refusal to admit he's wrong even while bleeding and fainting

jokes.text: I feel as hail and hearty as an eohippus.
jokes.setup: Burns describing his health
jokes.punchline: Using archaic language and referencing prehistoric horse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - archaic language with obscure paleontological reference

jokes.text: whooping cough, hectic fever, cancrum otis, Wellington's ooze, the Lambeth plague, and skull collapse
jokes.setup: Doctor listing Burns' ailments
jokes.punchline: Mix of real diseases and absurd made-up conditions
jokes.explanation: Escalating list mixing period-appropriate diseases with ridiculous inventions

jokes.text: You might live to see the Wheat-Eared Warbler return to Swinton Park. But I wouldn't count on it.
jokes.setup: Giving Burns a timeline for his remaining life
jokes.punchline: Using obscure birdwatching event as temporal marker
jokes.explanation: Beautifully specific and absurd way to measure remaining time - very British and antiquated

jokes.text: Whew! I will have to hand-crank my wiener warmer.
jokes.setup: Worrying about increased electric rates
jokes.punchline: Revealing he has an electric hot dog warmer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - obsessing over electronic gadget for simple food preparation

jokes.text: Hand-crank my wiener warmer!
jokes.setup: Repeating the phrase with emphasis
jokes.punchline: Double entendre becomes more apparent
jokes.explanation: Repetition for emphasis but adds sexual innuendo - slightly cheap but fits character

jokes.text: Finally, a break for the black man!
jokes.setup: Burns announcing he's dying
jokes.punchline: Carl celebrating death as racial progress
jokes.explanation: Carl's character perspective on Burns' oppression - dark but economical

jokes.text: My days are Roman-numeraled!
jokes.setup: Confirming he's really dying
jokes.punchline: Using antiquated numbering system instead of 'numbered'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - archaic and pompous way to say 'numbered,' shows his age and pretension

jokes.text: You are making a very powerful temporary enemy!
jokes.setup: Burns trying to threaten the celebrating crowd
jokes.punchline: Admitting his own powerlessness due to impending death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction - threat undermined by time limit, perfectly captures Burns' helpless rage

jokes.text: I'm leaving all my money to an orchard that will grow inedible 'me'-shaped fruits. They're as addictive as they are poisonous.
jokes.setup: Burns announcing his vindictive legacy
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan for posthumous fruit-based revenge
jokes.explanation: Incredibly specific and twisted legacy plan - perfectly Burns in its evil creativity and narcissism

jokes.text: I pay you, Smithers. No kind of love can come from one man paying another.
jokes.setup: Smithers saying Burns has him
jokes.punchline: Burns dismissing their relationship as purely transactional
jokes.explanation: Sad truth about Burns' inability to recognize genuine affection - reveals his emotional poverty

jokes.text: Shred them in the wood chipper, and use the rags to wax my car.
jokes.setup: Learning party guests' coats are on his bed
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Typical Burns escalation - destruction and recycling for petty revenge

jokes.text: crushing as many of those baby sea turtles as I possibly can
jokes.setup: Burns wanting to die on his own terms
jokes.punchline: Final act being environmentally destructive
jokes.explanation: Perfect final evil act for Burns - harming innocent animals for no reason

jokes.text: My poop stick has brought him back to life! But which end?
jokes.setup: Willie finding Burns after his fall
jokes.punchline: Uncertainty about whether Burns is right-side up
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy about Burns' skeletal frame making orientation unclear

jokes.text: My name Oatmeal?
jokes.setup: Willie saying Burns' brains turned to oatmeal
jokes.punchline: Burns adopting 'Oatmeal' as his name
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that establishes Burns' new childlike state

jokes.text: Ah, I'm playing with my Sergeant Activity Doll. DOLL: Why not buy another of me?
jokes.setup: Marge asking about noise in Bart's room
jokes.punchline: Toy's pre-recorded commercial message
jokes.explanation: Satirizes commercialized toys with built-in advertising messages

jokes.text: Because he would inject me with coffee at 6:00 a.m. in the back of the head.
jokes.setup: Smithers explaining why Burns was the reason he got up
jokes.punchline: Literal coffee injection as wake-up method
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd detail about Burns' extreme methods and Smithers' masochistic devotion

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. He likes to stack men naked.
jokes.setup: Describing new boss similarities to Burns
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Abu Ghraib scandal
jokes.explanation: Dark topical reference treated casually - somewhat cheap but economical

jokes.text: these are among the people who've come today to spit in Montgomery Burns' open grave
jokes.setup: Reporter describing mourners at Burns' funeral
jokes.punchline: Lists positive qualities then reveals they're there to desecrate the grave
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup with dark payoff about the nature of Burns' 'mourners'

jokes.text: I hope they use that spit for a good cause, like sealing wedding envelopes. Or helping a railroad worker grip his sledgehammer!
jokes.setup: People spitting in grave
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting practical uses for the spit
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - finding practical applications in disgusting situations

jokes.text: DOLL: Attack during their sacred holiday!
jokes.setup: Bart hiding Burns as toy continues talking
jokes.punchline: Toy giving violent military advice
jokes.explanation: Satirizes violent military toys with inappropriate messaging

jokes.text: He will do my bidding... at that fantasy football auction.
jokes.setup: Bart having evil control over Burns
jokes.punchline: Using power for mundane kid activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - child's limited imagination for using ultimate power

jokes.text: Marge, it's the golden rule: treat others the way they mess with you.
jokes.setup: Marge objecting to abusing helpless Burns
jokes.punchline: Homer's mangled version of the golden rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely misunderstanding fundamental moral principle

jokes.text: Eight-year-old? I always thought you were a midget! We're called 'little people,' and I'm not one.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on eight-year-old speaking up
jokes.punchline: Wiggum thinking Bart was a little person
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's confusion about Bart's age/size, with bonus PC correction

jokes.text: Yeah, Seaside Heights ain't Maui, but, uh, you take what you can get.
jokes.setup: Defending getting revenge on diminished Burns
jokes.punchline: Comparing revenge to settling for inferior vacation
jokes.explanation: Good analogy from Moe - settling for diminished revenge like a lesser vacation

jokes.text: What do you think I am, 1,000 years old? I got a myPod full of Lee Greenwood.
jokes.setup: Smithers mentioning Edison recordings
jokes.punchline: Cheney's age sensitivity and country music preference
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Cheney's defensiveness about age plus patriotic music preference

jokes.text: This town can't teach its kids or collect its garbage, but we lead the nation in pointless revenge.
jokes.setup: Organizing revenge schedule for Burns
jokes.punchline: Springfield's priorities revealed
jokes.explanation: Excellent social satire - town's dysfunction vs. their organizational skills for pettiness

jokes.text: Don't worry, boy. Another girder always swings in for you to walk on right when you get to the end.
jokes.setup: Homer making Burns walk construction beam
jokes.punchline: Homer's video game logic applied to real construction
jokes.explanation: Homer's video game logic creating dangerous real-world situation - perfect character moment

jokes.text: Oh, I can't shoot him. I've got him next. Well, that is your bad luck, because he is mine right now. Come back in half an hour. He can be your accomplice.
jokes.setup: Two people wanting to use Burns
jokes.punchline: Negotiating time-share for revenge victim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - treating Burns like a shared resource with scheduling conflicts

jokes.text: Found a way to use him as a bong.
jokes.setup: Bart asking what Otto did with Burns
jokes.punchline: Using elderly man as drug paraphernalia
jokes.explanation: Shocking and perfectly Otto - casual drug reference with human abuse

jokes.text: Yeah, I've done all I can do in that medium.
jokes.setup: Getting tired of revenge
jokes.punchline: Treating revenge as an artistic medium
jokes.explanation: Homer's artistic pretension about revenge - treating cruelty like a creative pursuit

jokes.text: Daddy's home.
jokes.setup: Burns recovering his memory in his mansion
jokes.punchline: Sinister return to power
jokes.explanation: Menacing two-word return to form - simple but effective character moment

jokes.text: And me smell like drugstore cologne the rest of the day? I don't think so. You may hug... my shadow.
jokes.setup: Smithers wanting to hug Burns upon his return
jokes.punchline: Burns offering shadow as compromise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - rejecting human contact while offering absurd substitute

jokes.text: You realize that every time I don't get what I want, Al Qaeda wins?
jokes.setup: Cheney learning Smithers is leaving
jokes.punchline: Connecting personal disappointment to national security
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cheney satire - making everything about terrorism for personal gain

jokes.text: I'm going to cover this town with a dome! It's been done! Really? You don't say. Did you know about this? Mm-hmm. I see.
jokes.setup: Burns threatening elaborate revenge
jokes.punchline: Learning his plan was already used in previous episode/movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke acknowledging The Simpsons Movie's dome plot - self-aware comedy

jokes.text: Plus we could always dig ourselves out, although that never seems to occur to anybody.
jokes.setup: Burns suggesting bulletproof cube prison
jokes.punchline: Homer pointing out obvious escape method
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on villain plot holes - characters becoming genre-aware

jokes.text: It wasn't kindness that was keeping me alive, it was bile building up inside!
jokes.setup: Theory that kindness kept Burns alive
jokes.punchline: Revealing hatred as actual life force
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character revelation - evil literally keeping him alive

jokes.text: Loathing is my life blood, and rage my royal jelly!
jokes.setup: Burns explaining what keeps him alive
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of hatred as sustenance
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns poetry - flowery language describing pure evil as nourishment

jokes.text: I won't be Bismarcked into fathering anyone!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to make Burns his father figure
jokes.punchline: Using historical figure as verb for political manipulation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns vocabulary - turning Otto von Bismarck into a verb for manipulation

jokes.text: My son? My son?! The truth is... I don't have a son!
jokes.setup: Bart mentioning Burns can't see his son acting up
jokes.punchline: Burns' dramatic revelation of childlessness
jokes.explanation: Burns' melodramatic reaction to obvious fact - treating childlessness like dark secret

tmdb_overview: Mr. Burns becomes distraught at the town's apathetic reaction to the announcement that he has only weeks to live; Bart secretly takes Burns into the family's home.
